fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 85 uses a strict greater-than comparison (>), which incorrectly considers an item valid when the current time is exactly equal to its TTL. It should use a greater-than-or-equal-to comparison (>=) to correctly expire items at their TTL boundary.

constraints.intent: The actual expiry logic should match the correct logic, which includes the equality case.
If an item is considered valid (not expired), the current time must be strictly less than its TTL.
A valid cache item must be a tuple of (payload, ttl), hence length 2.
The TTL timestamp must be a non-negative value.
